---
title: "Module Library"
---

# Reusable teaching modules

These pages **follow the same story as the slides**, but you can read them slowly on the website: good for **before class**, **catching up after a missed day**, or **review** before an exam.

We use **two kinds of examples**: **simulated genes** (we wrote the rules, so we know what is true) and **[Palmer Penguins](../dataset-penguins.qmd)** (real measurements you can plot and argue about).

::: {.callout-note appearance="simple"}
## Modules vs notebooks {#modules-vs-notebooks}

- **Modules** (this page, 01–08): **objectives-first** site pages — concepts, links to slides/data, and **short or canonical code** (especially **`tidymodels`** on penguins in **04** — train/test + Tuesday include — and **07–08**).
- **Notebooks** ([`notebooks/`](../notebooks/index.qmd)): **long IDE labs** — many chunks, heavy plots, **simulated genes**, and classical **`lm` / `glm` / `rpart` / `glmnet`** on penguins (knit locally or submit if the course asks).

**Format inventory (site spine)**

| Asset | Primary role |
|-------|----------------|
| [Home](../index.qmd) | Navigation |
| [Curriculum outline](../curriculum-outline.qmd) | Concept |
| [Monday slides](../slides/day-01-monday.qmd) | Concept |
| [Tuesday slides](../slides/day-02-tuesday.qmd) | Concept + **Canonical_code** (shared pipeline with Module 04) |
| [Day 4 slides](../slides/day-04-thursday.qmd) | Concept + code |
| [Notebook index](../notebooks/index.qmd) | **Lab_exploration** (+ homework when assigned) |

**Format inventory (modules 01–08)** — tag = main job of the page

| Module | Primary role |
|--------|----------------|
| [01](#module-01-foundations-and-generalization)–[03](#module-03-trees-and-two-cultures) | Concept |
| [04](#module-04-tidymodels-pipeline) | **Canonical_code** (train/test + full tuned pipeline; same include as Tuesday slides) |
| [05](#module-05-advanced-models)–[06](#module-06-metrics-imbalance-and-shap) | Concept |
| [07](#module-07-penguins-pick-your-metric)–[08](#module-08-penguins-compare-learners) | **Canonical_code** (metrics while tuning; compare engines) |
:::

| Module | Day focus | In plain language | Role |
|--------|-----------|-------------------|------|
| [01 — Foundations](#module-01-foundations-and-generalization) | Monday | Train vs test, “too wiggly” models, one-page **data description** habit | Concept |
| [02 — Regularization](#module-02-regularization) | Monday | Ridge, lasso, and stepwise/AIC — when to shrink coefficients or drop predictors | Concept |
| [03 — Trees & cultures](#module-03-trees-and-two-cultures) | Tuesday | Two ways of thinking about statistics + **decision trees** (“if–then” rules) | Concept |
| [04 — Modeling pipeline in R](#module-04-tidymodels-pipeline) | Tuesday | **Train/test + `glm`** first, then the **same tuned-tree code as Tuesday’s slides** (shared include; `tidymodels`) | Canonical_code |
| [05 — Advanced models](#module-05-advanced-models) | Thursday | Stronger “black box” models (random forest, boosting, neural nets) — **same checking discipline**, bigger capacity | Concept |
| [06 — Metrics & explanations](#module-06-metrics-imbalance-and-shap) | Thursday | Picking scores that match the biology/clinic question, imbalance, and reading **SHAP** plots carefully | Concept |
| [07 — Pick your metric](#module-07-penguins-pick-your-metric) | Tuesday / lab | **Tune once**, track **accuracy**, **kappa**, and **ROC AUC**; see how `select_best()` changes when you change the metric | Canonical_code |
| [08 — Compare learners](#module-08-penguins-compare-learners) | Thursday / lab | Same folds + recipe, compare **random forest**, **XGBoost**, **small MLP** (`fit_resamples`) | Canonical_code |

---

## Module 01: Foundations and Generalization {#module-01-foundations-and-generalization}

**Predicting an outcome from measurements**, splitting data so you are honest about performance, and the “too simple vs too wiggly” picture you see on Monday. Links **gene simulations** and **penguins** so the same ideas appear on two tables.

**[Open Module 01 →](module-01-foundations.qmd)**

---

## Module 02: Regularization {#module-02-regularization}

**Ridge** and **lasso** gently pull coefficients toward zero; **AIC / stepwise** search different subsets of predictors. Connects Monday’s slides to the **gene LASSO** notebook and **penguin body-mass** shrinkage practice.

**[Open Module 02 →](module-02-regularization.qmd)**

---

## Module 03: Trees and Two Cultures {#module-03-trees-and-two-cultures}

Two traditions in statistics (interpretable models vs flexible prediction), then **decision trees**: sequential splits, depth, and what a **tree diagram** means for penguins.

**[Open Module 03 →](module-03-trees-and-cultures.qmd)**

---

## Module 04: Modeling pipeline in R {#module-04-tidymodels-pipeline}

**[Train/test + `glm` →](module-04-pipeline.qmd#train-test-last-fit)** then the **[same tuned-tree code as Tuesday’s deck](module-04-pipeline.qmd#canonical-pipeline-tuesday)** (shared include so slides and this page **never drift apart**).

**[Open Module 04 →](module-04-pipeline.qmd)**

---

## Module 05: Advanced models {#module-05-advanced-models}

Random forests, boosting, and neural nets as **more flexible** learners. Emphasis: keep **cross-validation and metrics** strict, and **swap the type of model** in the same overall workflow you learned on Tuesday.

**[Open Module 05 →](module-05-advanced-models.qmd)**

---

## Module 06: Metrics, imbalance, and SHAP {#module-06-metrics-imbalance-and-shap}

Choosing **numbers that match the decision** (miss a case vs call a false alarm), **class imbalance**, and using **SHAP** and importance plots as **descriptions** — not as proof you should intervene in a biological system.

**[Open Module 06 →](module-06-evaluation-and-interpretability.qmd)**

---

## Module 07: Pick the score you care about {#module-07-penguins-pick-your-metric}

One **tuned** classification tree on the same Adelie vs Gentoo table, but **three metrics at once** (accuracy, Cohen’s kappa, ROC AUC) so you can see **`select_best()`** pick different hyperparameters when you change the optimization target.

**[Open Module 07 →](module-07-penguins-choose-metrics.qmd)**

---

## Module 08: Compare a few strong models {#module-08-penguins-compare-learners}

Hold the **recipe and folds** fixed; swap **random forest**, **XGBoost**, and a **small neural net** — fair **`fit_resamples`** comparison on the same penguin task used in Modules 04 and 07.

**[Open Module 08 →](module-08-penguins-compare-models.qmd)**
